description_long: This barn is a tall, rectangular structure with vertical wooden siding and a prominent monitor-style roof. The roof features a raised central section with windows on all sides, allowing for increased ventilation and natural light. The main body of the barn is clad in vertical planks, and there are small, square doors or hatches on the front and rear, as well as a larger access door at the base. The upper section includes a black, cylindrical element, possibly a ventilation drum or storage tank, partially enclosed by a railing. Decorative cross-bracing is visible at the roof's peak, adding to the barn's traditional appearance. The overall design suggests a utilitarian agricultural building adapted for urban or peri-urban settings.
description: A tall, monitor-roof barn with vertical wood siding, upper windows, and a cylindrical feature on top. It has multiple access doors and decorative roof bracing.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main access door, vertical siding, and the cylindrical feature atop the roof, partially enclosed by a railing.
description_view_1: Rear view highlights the monitor roof's window arrangement and decorative cross-bracing at the peak, with no large access door visible.
description_view_2: Side view similar to the front, with the cylindrical feature and railing more prominent, and vertical siding uninterrupted.
description_view_3: Opposite rear view shows the monitor roof, cross-bracing, and a small hatch at the base, with no large door.
attribute_roof_type: monitor roof with a raised central section and gable ends
attribute_door_style: single hinged main door with additional small square hatches
attribute_window_arrangement: continuous row of windows in the raised monitor section, evenly spaced on all sides
